title: weinmannia tinctoria f. paucifoliolata
intro: Weinmannia tinctoria f. paucifoliolata, also known as the 'Paucifoliolata' or 'Paraguay copal', is a lesser-known variant of the Weinmannia tinctoria plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Weinmannia tinctoria f. paucifoliolata are diverse, including the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's resin is often extracted and used in traditional medicines, such as ointments, salves, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties.
